titleStmt.title: Letter from Stephen Brown to Henry Arthur Wynne, 19 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Stephen Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter belonging to on-going correspondence between Henry Arthur Wynne and Crown Solicitor for Kildare, Stephen Brown (1854-1931) regarding a leave of absence. Browne inquires as to the correct form of application for his leave.In 1916 Henry Arthur Wynne (1867-1945) assumed the position of chief crown solicitor for Ireland following the death of Malachy Kelly (1850-1916).

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, NAI-CSO-RP-1916-5618-12591-3

correspAction.placeName: Crown Solicitors Office, Naas, County Kildare, Ireland
The Chief Crown Solicitor, Dublin Castle, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Crown Solicitor's Office, Naas, 19th July 1916. Dear Sir I am anxious to obtain
leave of absence for a three week
vacation commencing about 2nd
August and shall feel obliged if
you will kindly have sent to me
the necessary form of application. Faithfully Yours Stephen Brown The Chief Crown Soli. Castle, Dublin.
